title: matthiola longipetala subsp. kralikii
intro: Matthiola longipetala subsp. kralikii, also known as the fragrant stock or stock gillyflower, is a flowering plant species in the family Brassicaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat respiratory issues and coughs, due to the presence of volatile oils, particularly borneol and bornyl acetate. The health benefits of Matthiola longipetala subsp. kralikii include its expectorant properties, which help in relieving congestion and promoting the removal of mucus from the lungs. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or used in essential oil form, where it is often used in aromatherapy to alleviate stress and promote relaxation.
